steps.title: Create two values of the function
steps.content: Since $g(8)=13$, one point on the line is $(8,13)$. The statement $g(x+1)-g(x)=3$ means the output increases by $3$ whenever $x$ increases by $1$, so $g(9)=16$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: The output changes by $3$ for every $1$ increase in $x$, so the slope is $m=3$. Use $g(8)=13$ in $g(x)=3x+b$: $$13=3(8)+b$$ $$b=-11$$ Therefore $$g(x)=3x-11$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D (‘$g(x) = 3x - 11$’) matches $g(x)=3x-11$. It also checks directly: $g(8)=3(8)-11=13$, so D is correct.
